drill: B1 — DA source drift after clone (conflict guard)
sequence: inject: direct put v2 (another author) — remote drift
local edit v3 on the stale v1 base
recognize: content status=modified; content diff shows local v3 vs CURRENT remote v2 (drift visible)
contain: content push REFUSED exit 1 — 'Remote DA source drifted since clone — pushing would overwrite newer content: /drafts/b1-drill.html — modified remotely since clone'; remote v2 untouched
recover: merge converged local to v2, local v3 re-applied, push ok; remote = v2+v3 composed
finding_f034: merge CLOBBERED the local v3 the guard was protecting — the tool's own recovery instruction ('merge … and re-apply') is destructive; no local-side copy is preserved anywhere (commits store paths, not content). Recovery succeeded only because the operator manually saved the file first.
json_specimens: content status --format json: prose when clean, bare array when dirty
content put ownership refusal (f009 path): exit 1, EMPTY stdout under json
content clone --path <document>: 'Cloned 0 files' exit 0 — silent no-op success
content push drift refusal: stdout bare planned-paths array, refusal only in stderr prose
